stem: If 3x + 5 = 20, what is the value of x?
Machine A completes a job 20% faster than Machine B. If Machine B takes 60 minutes, how many minutes does Machine A take?
explanation: A 20% faster completion rate means time falls to 60 / 1.2 = 50 minutes.

stem:
Is integer n even?
(1) n is divisible by 2.
(2) n is divisible by 4.
options: Statement (1) alone is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not sufficient.
Statement (2) alone is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not sufficient.
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither statement alone is sufficient.
Each statement alone is sufficient.
Statements (1) and (2) together are not sufficient.
explanation: Either statement guarantees n is even, so each alone is sufficient.

options: Statement (1) alone is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not sufficient.
Statement (2) alone is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not sufficient.
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither statement alone is sufficient.
Each statement alone is sufficient.
Statements (1) and (2) together are not sufficient.
explanation: Statement (1) gives x = 3 or -3, so it is not sufficient. Statement (2) simplifies to x > 0, so it is sufficient.

stem: City bike-share usage fell after docking fees rose. The city council argues that cutting docking fees will restore ridership and reduce traffic congestion. Which statement most strengthens the council's argument?
options: A nearby city reduced docking fees and saw both ridership increase and short car trips decline.
Some residents prefer walking to cycling even when fees are low.
The city plans to repaint several bike lanes next year.
Docking fees account for a small share of total city revenue.
explanation: Evidence from a similar city directly supports the claim that lower fees can boost ridership and reduce traffic.

stem: A company wants to replace live training workshops with recorded video lessons to save money while maintaining training effectiveness. Which assumption does the argument require?
options: The company has already reduced travel spending in other departments.
Employees will complete and engage with the recorded lessons sufficiently to learn the material.
Recorded lessons are cheaper to produce than to stream online.
Managers prefer asynchronous learning to live instruction.
explanation: The plan only works if employees actually use the recorded training enough to preserve effectiveness.

stem:
Choose the best version of the sentence.
Unlike the previous quarter, revenue from subscriptions, together with service fees, were enough to offset hardware declines.
options: were enough to offset hardware declines
was enough to offset hardware declines
have been enough in offsetting hardware declines
were enough for offsetting hardware declines
explanation: The subject is singular, revenue, so the correct verb is was.

stem:
Choose the best version of the sentence.
Neither the consultants nor the CEO were willing to delay the vote.
options: were willing to delay the vote
was willing to delay the vote
have been willing to delay the vote
are willing to delay the vote
explanation: With neither/nor, the verb agrees with the nearer subject, CEO, which is singular.

stem:
Passage: Adaptive tests estimate ability by selecting later questions based on earlier responses. Because of this design, two students may see different questions but still receive comparable scores.

The main point of the passage is that adaptive tests:
options: are less fair because students see different questions
compare students by using different questions selected from a random pool
can produce comparable scores even when students answer different questions
should replace all fixed-form exams immediately
explanation: The passage explains that adaptive testing can still compare students fairly despite different question sets.

stem: Five consecutive integers have a sum of 85. What is the greatest integer?
explanation: The average is 85 / 5 = 17, so the integers are 15, 16, 17, 18, and 19.

options: Statement (1) alone is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not sufficient.
Statement (2) alone is sufficient, but statement (1) alone is not sufficient.
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither statement alone is sufficient.
Each statement alone is sufficient.
Statements (1) and (2) together are not sufficient.
explanation: Statement (1) simplifies directly to y < 3. Statement (2) implies -3 < y < 3, which is also sufficient.

stem: A manager concludes that a new dashboard improved team productivity because output rose after the dashboard launched. Which fact would most weaken that conclusion?
options: The team had already hired two additional analysts shortly before output increased.
The dashboard uses a more modern color palette than the previous tool.
Some employees said they liked the dashboard layout.
The dashboard can be viewed on mobile devices.
explanation: A simultaneous staffing increase offers an alternative explanation for higher output and therefore weakens the causal claim.
